---
title: "Simon Del Villar"
description: ""
about:
    template: trestles
    links:
        - icon: linkedin
          text: LinkedIn
          url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/simon-del-villar-80b12a27b/
        - icon: github
          text: Github
          url: https://github.com/simondelvillar
        - icon: instagram
          text: Instagram
          url: https://www.instagram.com/simondelvillar/
        - text: "{{< fa envelope >}} Email"
          url: mailto:simon.joven.delvillar@gmail.com
image: img/simonphoto.jpg
comments: false
---

# Bio 
Hello! I'm Simon. When I’m not buried under a pile of math homework or deep in a textbook, I’m usually enjoying the outdoors, taking photos with my photography friends, and spending time with the people I love.

I’m a math student, but I’m a 'generalist' at heart. I find joy in the variety of mathematics—from the abstract puzzles that keep me up at night to the data-driven projects that help me make sense of the world.

I built this site to bridge the gap between my academic life and the things that genuinely interest me. Grab a coffee (or tea, or water!), browse my latest projects, and if you want to talk shop—or just talk about stuff—let's connect.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
### 📌 Currently Featured

**Wealth, Identity, and the 2022 Philippine Election**

Why did provinces vote the way they did in one of the most consequential elections in modern Philippine history?

This project combines electoral results from the 2022 presidential election with regional economic indicators from the Philippine Statistics Authority to explore whether wealth, development, and geography influenced voting patterns. While some of the wealthiest regions supported Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and others favored Leni Robredo, the data reveals a more nuanced story shaped by regional identity, historical memory, and local political dynamics.

Using maps, visualizations, and statistical analysis, the project investigates a central political economy question:

> *Can economic conditions explain electoral behaviour, or are culture and history equally important?*

[View project →](projects/political-economy.qmd)
:::

# Beyond Mathematics
- 📷 Landscape and nature photography (check out [my photography site!](https://simondelvillar.github.io/simondvphotography/))
- 🏔️ Exploring local trails and outdoor spaces
- 💻 Programming and personal coding projects
- 📊 Data visualization and analytics
- 🏃 Staying active through sports and recreation
- 📚 Learning new skills and exploring new ideas
- Don't forget food!


<div class="listening-now">
🎧 Currently listening to <em>Strangers in the Night</em> — Bert Kaempfert
</div>


## Behind the Scenes

Most of this website is built and maintained through Git, GitHub, and Quarto.

<div class="commit-map">
  <img
    src="https://github-readme-stats.vercel.app/api?username=simondelvillar&show_icons=true&hide_border=true&rank_icon=github"
    alt="GitHub statistics">
</div>
